mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 55% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with p > 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decreases in consumer prices indicate strong real demand without inflationary pressure, boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return differential greater than 0.5% compared to baseline, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects expanding capacity utilization and rising factory output, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and short-term gains.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during easing periods, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with t-stat < 1.5 when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and higher household income, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with equity returns with Rank IC < 0.01 across multiple market cycles, or positive returns drop below 50% in high-growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 900,000 units signal a contraction in construction activity and reduced household wealth effects, leading to negative equity market performance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in over 65% of instances when housing starts breach the 900 threshold, invalidating the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling federal funds rates and declining unemployment creates an optimal growth environment where cheap capital meets a strengthening labor market, producing strong positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields a negative or zero return differential, or the joint condition fails to outperform single-series signals in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth surpassing 3.0% confirms robust economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, which sustains investor confidence and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Returns turn negative in more than 40% of quarters where GDP growth exceeds 3.0%, or the signal shows no predictive edge in out-of-sample periods.
